title: When Galaxies Clash
gist: A new astronomical image of colliding galaxies is presented as a possible preview of the Milky Way's future, framed by the extreme speeds at which Earth and the galaxy are already moving through space.

insights.claim: Earth and the entire solar system orbit the Milky Way's central black hole at about 514,000 miles per hour.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Vivid scale of everyday cosmic motion.

insights.claim: Subtracting the sun's rotation around the galactic center, the Milky Way itself is traveling at roughly 1.4 million mph outward, riding the expansion from the Big Bang.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Quantifies the galaxy's bulk motion through the universe.

insights.claim: A newly released image of colliding star systems is framed as a possible vision of the Milky Way's own future collisions.
insights.speaker: Bob Grant
insights.so_what: Galaxy mergers as the long-run fate of our own.
insights.locator: Is a new image of star systems
